fields.subject: Listen to the voicemail below or read the transcript. Is this a legitimate call?
fields.body: Hello, this is Microsoft Windows Support. We are detecting a severe virus originating from your IP address. Please hold the line to connect with a certified technician.

fields.feedback_text: Correct! This is a malicious phishing attempt (Tech support virus scam). Legitimate organizations like banks, police, or tech support will never call you out of the blue and ask you to 'press 1' to resolve a severe issue or verify your PIN. This creates a false sense of extreme urgency.

Incorrect. This is a malicious phishing attempt (Tech support virus scam). Legitimate organizations like banks, police, or tech support will never call you out of the blue and ask you to 'press 1' to resolve a severe issue or verify your PIN. This creates a false sense of extreme urgency.

fields.subject: Listen to the voicemail below or read the transcript. Is this a legitimate call?
fields.body: Hi, this is the DPD driver. I'm outside your building but the gate is locked. Could you pop down or give me the entry code? Thanks.

fields.feedback_text: Incorrect. This is a legitimate automated call (Legitimate delivery driver call). Notice that it is only providing information (like a reminder) and is NOT asking for sensitive information, payments, or demanding immediate action to avoid a penalty.

fields.subject: Listen to the voicemail below or read the transcript. Is this a legitimate call?
fields.body: Hello, your Amazon account has a pending charge of €899 for a new iPhone. If you did not authorize this purchase, press 1 now to connect to the fraud department.

fields.feedback_text: Correct! This is a malicious phishing attempt (Amazon pending charge scam). Legitimate organizations like banks, police, or tech support will never call you out of the blue and ask you to 'press 1' to resolve a severe issue or verify your PIN. This creates a false sense of extreme urgency.

Incorrect. This is a malicious phishing attempt (Amazon pending charge scam). Legitimate organizations like banks, police, or tech support will never call you out of the blue and ask you to 'press 1' to resolve a severe issue or verify your PIN. This creates a false sense of extreme urgency.
